title: Changing the date in the main and sub-orders
excerpt: Does anyone know how to make a tip so that when I change the date in the main order, the date in the subordinate operation changes automatically?

posts.author: Drakolicz1 (Patryk)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know how to make a tip so that when I change the date in the main order, the date in the subordinate operation changes automatically?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: To confirm, you’re asking about changing the date in a parent item to affect the subitems?

posts.author: james598patton (James Patton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Drakolicz1:

Does anyone know how to make a tip so that when I change the date in the main order, the date in the subordinate operation changes automatically?

Hello @Drakolicz1,
To ensure subordinate operation dates update automatically with the main order date, enable dependency-based scheduling in your system. Most ERP platforms support this via linked operations or scheduling parameters. Check that operations are properly linked and set to inherit changes from the main order.

Best Regards,
James Patton

posts.author: kyla598ellis (Kyla Ellis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Drakolicz1:

Does anyone know how to make a tip so that when I change the date in the main order, the date in the subordinate operation changes automatically?

Hello @Drakolicz1,
To sync subordinate operation dates with the main order date, enable automatic scheduling in your system settings. Most ERP platforms (like SAP or Dynamics) offer this via scheduling parameters or dependency rules. Check your order configuration to ensure operations are linked and set to inherit changes from the header. myaarpmedicare

Best Regards,
Kyla Ellis
